_comment: WARNING: Although this file was initially generated by the Pterodactyl Panel, it has since been manually edited to support multiple Postgres versions and upgrade logic. Do not modify unless you understand the full impact of your changes.
name: Postgres (13–18)
author: itzzmateo@devflare.de
description: Postgres with multi-version support (13–18) and safe upgrade handling.

installation.script:
#!/bin/ash

adduser -D -h /home/container container
chown -R container: /mnt/server/

DATA_DIR="/mnt/server/postgres_db"
RUN_DIR="$DATA_DIR/run"
NEW_VERSION="${PG_VERSION}"

mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"

if [ -f "$DATA_DIR/PG_VERSION" ]; then
    OLD_VERSION=$(cat "$DATA_DIR/PG_VERSION")
else
    OLD_VERSION=""
fi

echo "Old version: $OLD_VERSION"
echo "New version: $NEW_VERSION"

# Fresh install
if [ -z "$OLD_VERSION" ]; then
    echo "Initializing new database..."
    su container -c "initdb -D $DATA_DIR -U \"$PGUSER\" --pwfile=<(echo \"$PGPASSWORD\")"

else
    if [ "$OLD_VERSION" != "$NEW_VERSION" ]; then
        echo "Version change detected. Creating backup..."

        BACKUP_DIR="/mnt/server/backup_$(date +%s)"
        cp -r "$DATA_DIR" "$BACKUP_DIR"
        echo "Backup created at $BACKUP_DIR"

        echo "Attempting pg_upgrade..."

        OLD_BIN="/usr/lib/postgresql/$OLD_VERSION/bin"
        NEW_BIN="/usr/lib/postgresql/$NEW_VERSION/bin"

        if [ -x "$OLD_BIN/pg_ctl" ] && [ -x "$NEW_BIN/pg_ctl" ]; then
            su container -c "pg_upgrade \
                -b $OLD_BIN \
                -B $NEW_BIN \
                -d $DATA_DIR \
                -D ${DATA_DIR}_new"

            if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
                echo "Upgrade successful"
                rm -rf "$DATA_DIR"
                mv "${DATA_DIR}_new" "$DATA_DIR"
            else
                echo "pg_upgrade failed. Keeping old data."
            fi
        else
            echo "pg_upgrade not possible (missing binaries)."
            echo "Keeping old database. Manual upgrade required."
        fi
    else
        echo "Version unchanged. Skipping upgrade."
    fi
fi

# Ensure pg_hba rule
if ! grep -q "# Custom rules" "$DATA_DIR/pg_hba.conf"; then
    echo -e "# Custom rules\nhost    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               md5" >> "$DATA_DIR/pg_hba.conf"
fi

echo "Done"

variables.name: Superuser Name
variables.description: The username for the postgres superuser

variables.name: Superuser Password
variables.description:
The postgres super user password with a strong default.
You should be generating new ones for each server.
If you don't then users can hit other users DB's

variables.name: Postgres Version
variables.description: Select the Postgres version to use
